graph.topic: Understanding the daily workflows, operational bottlenecks, and core professional challenges of elderly care workers in home visit and community center settings.
graph.researcher_bg: An HCI researcher specializing in workplace technologies and caregiving systems. The researcher aims to understand the daily operational burdens and workflow inefficiencies of elderly care professionals but lacks firsthand experience in frontline care management.
graph.expert_bg: A senior community care coordinator who manages complex caregiver workflows and daily operations at a community center. The expert possesses deep firsthand knowledge of the administrative burdens, emotional labor, and logistical challenges that care professionals face daily.

q_01__shared_root.label: Understanding the daily workflows, operational bottlenecks, and core professional challenges of elderly care workers in home visit and community center settings.
q_01__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Understanding the daily workflows, operational bottlenecks, and core professional challenges of elderly care workers in home visit and community center settings.

q_01__exp_L1_00.label: Senior Community Care Coordinator
q_01__exp_L1_00.description: The administrative and emotional reality of managing caregiver workflows, family expectations, and operational continuity in home and community care.

q_01__exp_L2_00_00.label: Operational Workflow & Admin Burden
q_01__exp_L2_00_00.description: The structural and administrative friction that consumes actual care time, often invisible to outsiders.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Communication Intake Overload
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: Starting the day drowning in emails and voicemails from caregivers, spending the first hour just triaging who needs what before actual work begins.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Dynamic Scheduling & Routing
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: Mapping out visits and routes, then scrambling to reassign everything when a caregiver calls in sick or traffic hits a specific bottleneck.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_02.label: Reactive Documentation Lag
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_02.description: The paperwork always trails the actual care; spending the end of the day chasing staff to upload logs so you aren't non-compliant tomorrow.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_03.label: Care Plan Compliance
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_03.description: Pausing care because a family member hasn't signed the updated plan yet, forcing you to nag them legally before the visit can start.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_04.label: Resource & Supply Oversight
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_04.description: Worrying that the caregiver left with the wrong gauze size or missed a supply pack, forcing a mid-visit pharmacy run you have to authorize.

q_01__exp_L2_00_01.label: Client & Family Relational Dynamics
q_01__exp_L2_00_01.description: The emotional labor and interpersonal negotiations required to deliver care effectively.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Family Expectation Management
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: Families asking for small favors outside the care plan that add up, or calling constantly for updates that interrupt your coordination flow.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Inter-Party Mediation
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: Being the middleman between what the caregiver reports, what the family expects, and what the center allows, smoothing over friction before it becomes a complaint.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_02.label: Detecting Caregiver & Family Strain
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_02.description: Hearing the tone in a daughter's voice or a caregiver's report change over weeks, signaling overwhelm before they actually admit it.

q_01__exp_L2_00_02.label: Tacit Coordination & Continuity
q_01__exp_L2_00_02.description: The unwritten workflows and observational skills that keep care safe when formal systems fail.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_00.label: Informal Knowledge Reliance
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_00.description: Trusting the handwritten note on the fridge more than the electronic log because it holds the real rules like 'don't knock too loud'.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_01.label: Blurred Professional Boundaries
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_01.description: Not having a real start or end to the shift, just a steady rhythm of triaging emergencies where you're never truly off the clock.

q_01__shared_root.label: Understanding the daily workflows, operational bottlenecks, and core professional challenges of elderly care workers in home visit and community center settings.
q_01__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Understanding the daily workflows, operational bottlenecks, and core professional challenges of elderly care workers in home visit and community center settings.

q_01__res_L1_00.label: Temporal Task Architecture
q_01__res_L1_00.description: Analyzing the chronological structure of the workday to understand how time is allocated across different phases of care and logistics.

q_01__res_L2_00_00.label: Shift Phase Distribution
q_01__res_L2_00_00.description: Investigating how the total shift duration is segmented into preparatory, active care, and administrative blocks.

q_01__res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Quantify care vs. admin ratio
q_01__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: Determine the percentage of shift time dedicated to direct client interaction versus preparatory routines and closing documentation.

q_01__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Identify peak density windows
q_01__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Map specific clock hours where task density significantly exceeds the shift average to locate high-pressure intervals.

q_01__res_L2_00_01.label: Physical Logistics Overhead
q_01__res_L2_00_01.description: Measuring the time cost associated with physical movement required to deliver care in different settings.

q_01__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Measure transit time proportion
q_01__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Calculate the proportion of total shift duration consumed by travel between home visit locations.

q_01__res_leaf_00_01_01.label: Map intra-site navigation duration
q_01__res_leaf_00_01_01.description: Record the time spent navigating physical spaces within a community center before direct care delivery begins.

q_01__res_L1_01.label: Cognitive Flow & Continuity
q_01__res_L1_01.description: Examining the stability of the worker's focus and the frequency of disruptions that fragment the workflow.

q_01__res_L2_01_00.label: Interruption Frequency
q_01__res_L2_01_00.description: Tracking how often external stimuli cause involuntary suspensions of the primary task.

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Count context switches per hour
q_01__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Track the frequency of task suspensions caused by external stimuli during direct care periods.

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Categorize interruption sources
q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Classify interruptions by origin (e.g., technology, family, colleague) to identify dominant flow breakers.

q_01__res_L2_01_01.label: Recovery Latency
q_01__res_L2_01_01.description: Estimating the time required to regain full cognitive focus after a workflow disruption occurs.

q_01__res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Estimate re-orientation time
q_01__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Assess the latency period required to re-orient to the primary task following an administrative interruption.

q_01__res_leaf_01_01_01.label: Identify flow state barriers
q_01__res_leaf_01_01_01.description: Pinpoint specific workflow steps where maintaining continuous focus is most difficult due to process design.

q_01__res_L1_02.label: Artifact & Information Ecology
q_01__res_L1_02.description: Understanding when and how workers interact with tools, records, and communication channels throughout the day.

q_01__res_L2_02_00.label: Documentation Timing
q_01__res_L2_02_00.description: Analyzing the temporal relationship between care delivery and data entry tasks.

q_01__res_leaf_02_00_00.label: Determine entry synchronicity
q_01__res_leaf_02_00_00.description: Determine the ratio of documentation performed concurrently with care versus post-shift batching.

q_01__res_L2_02_01.label: Handoff Protocols
q_01__res_L2_02_01.description: Mapping the standard procedures and tools used for transferring responsibility between shifts.

q_01__res_leaf_02_01_00.label: Chronometer transfer duration
q_01__res_leaf_02_01_00.description: Measure the duration required to complete standard end-of-shift information transfer protocols.

assistance_payload.validate_focus: The expert emphasizes how communication intake overload creates a critical bottleneck at the start of the shift. This qualitative barrier prevents actual care work from beginning efficiently.

pivot.limitation: Focusing solely on the intake bottleneck misses the broader distribution of time across the entire shift. It does not capture how much total time is lost to administrative tasks versus direct care throughout the day.
pivot.research_goal: The researcher aims to quantify the ratio of direct client interaction to preparatory and documentation routines. This provides an aggregate view of operational efficiency beyond just the morning triage.
pivot.compelling_reason: While the intake overload is a vital pain point, understanding the overall care vs. admin ratio helps identify if this bottleneck is an isolated incident or part of a systemic imbalance affecting total care delivery. Both perspectives are needed to fully map the workflow challenges.
pivot.coarse_scenario: I hear you that drowning in emails first thing blocks everything else, and that's critical. But if we can also pin down how that morning scramble compares to your admin time throughout the rest of the shift, it helps us prove whether this is just a startup issue or a systemic staffing imbalance.

probes.question: Is the risk of error higher when boxes are similar-shaped or when names are similar-sounding?
Which omission causes more worry: missing the primary dressing or missing the secondary tape?
Is the burden worse due to the time delay involved or the legal liability incurred?
Does your oversight intensity relax after 6 months, 1 year, or 3 years of employment?
Is the worry triggered more by routine cleaning tasks or complex wound vac setups?
Does the risk of error increase during the first 15 minutes of a shift or the last 15 minutes?
Are mistakes more common due to small font size on labels or low contrast colors on boxes?
Do you trust a digital photo upload, a physical signature, or a verbal phone call more?

hypothetical_scenarios.scenario: If Supply Item Verification changed from similar-shaped boxes to distinct shapes, would your judgment about 'Resource & Supply Oversight' change?
If Pack Completeness changed from separate items to pre-bundled kits, would your judgment about 'Resource & Supply Oversight' change?
If Authorization Friction changed from manual approval to auto-approval for veterans, would your judgment about 'Resource & Supply Oversight' change?
If Caregiver Tenure changed from 1 month to 5 years, would your judgment about 'Resource & Supply Oversight' change?
If Patient Care Complexity changed from complex wound vacs to standard saline, would your judgment about 'Resource & Supply Oversight' change?
If Departure Time Pressure changed from a 15-minute window to a 45-minute window, would your judgment about 'Resource & Supply Oversight' change?
If Label Distinguishability changed from small font to color-coded boxes, would your judgment about 'Resource & Supply Oversight' change?
If Confirmation Method changed from physical signature to barcode scan, would your judgment about 'Resource & Supply Oversight' change?

q_01__exp_L1_00.label: Senior Community Care Coordinator
q_01__exp_L1_00.description: The administrative and emotional reality of managing caregiver workflows, family expectations, and operational continuity in home and community care.

q_01__exp_L2_00_00.label: Operational Workflow & Admin Burden
q_01__exp_L2_00_00.description: The structural and administrative friction that consumes actual care time, often invisible to outsiders.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Communication Intake Overload
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: Starting the day drowning in emails and voicemails from caregivers, spending the first hour just triaging who needs what before actual work begins.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Dynamic Scheduling & Routing
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: Mapping out visits and routes, then scrambling to reassign everything when a caregiver calls in sick or traffic hits a specific bottleneck.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_02.label: Reactive Documentation Lag
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_02.description: The paperwork always trails the actual care; spending the end of the day chasing staff to upload logs so you aren't non-compliant tomorrow.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_03.label: Care Plan Compliance
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_03.description: Pausing care because a family member hasn't signed the updated plan yet, forcing you to nag them legally before the visit can start.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_04.label: Resource & Supply Oversight
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_04.description: Worrying that the caregiver left with the wrong gauze size or missed a supply pack, forcing a mid-visit pharmacy run you have to authorize.

q_01__exp_L2_00_01.label: Client & Family Relational Dynamics
q_01__exp_L2_00_01.description: The emotional labor and interpersonal negotiations required to deliver care effectively.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Family Expectation Management
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: Families asking for small favors outside the care plan that add up, or calling constantly for updates that interrupt your coordination flow.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Inter-Party Mediation
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: Being the middleman between what the caregiver reports, what the family expects, and what the center allows, smoothing over friction before it becomes a complaint.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_02.label: Detecting Caregiver & Family Strain
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_02.description: Hearing the tone in a daughter's voice or a caregiver's report change over weeks, signaling overwhelm before they actually admit it.

q_01__exp_L2_00_02.label: Tacit Coordination & Continuity
q_01__exp_L2_00_02.description: The unwritten workflows and observational skills that keep care safe when formal systems fail.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_00.label: Informal Knowledge Reliance
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_00.description: Trusting the handwritten note on the fridge more than the electronic log because it holds the real rules like 'don't knock too loud'.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_01.label: Blurred Professional Boundaries
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_01.description: Not having a real start or end to the shift, just a steady rhythm of triaging emergencies where you're never truly off the clock.

assistance_payload.validate_focus: The expert emphasizes how communication intake overload creates a critical bottleneck at the start of the shift. This qualitative barrier prevents actual care work from beginning efficiently.

pivot.limitation: Focusing solely on the intake bottleneck misses the broader distribution of time across the entire shift. It does not capture how much total time is lost to administrative tasks versus direct care throughout the day.
pivot.research_goal: The researcher aims to quantify the ratio of direct client interaction to preparatory and documentation routines. This provides an aggregate view of operational efficiency beyond just the morning triage.
pivot.compelling_reason: While the intake overload is a vital pain point, understanding the overall care vs. admin ratio helps identify if this bottleneck is an isolated incident or part of a systemic imbalance affecting total care delivery. Both perspectives are needed to fully map the workflow challenges.
pivot.coarse_scenario: I hear you that drowning in emails first thing blocks everything else, and that's critical. But if we can also pin down how that morning scramble compares to your admin time throughout the rest of the shift, it helps us prove whether this is just a startup issue or a systemic staffing imbalance.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: You show up and the pill organizer looks nothing like the pharmacy label because a doctor changed a dose last week and nobody told us.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: The 'Pill Count' Gut Check
q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: You learn to spot non-adherence not by counting pills, but by noticing the box is turned slightly differently or the client avoids eye contact when you ask about their morning dose.

q_02__exp_L2_00_01.label: Environmental Safety
q_02__exp_L2_00_01.description: Navigating physical risks in the home that aren't captured on standard checklists.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Hidden Home Hazards
q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: The intake form says 'safe home', but you get there and there's a loose rug or dim lighting that wasn't flagged until someone almost tripped.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Dignity vs. Safety Trade-off
q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: Knowing when to push for a walker versus letting them walk unassisted because forcing it makes them refuse all care just to prove they're still independent.

q_02__exp_L1_01.label: Relational Friction & Communication
q_02__exp_L1_01.description: Managing the emotional and professional boundaries between families, caregivers, and the center.

q_02__exp_L2_01_00.label: Family Boundaries
q_02__exp_L2_01_00.description: Handling family requests that cross into medical advice or unrealistic expectations.

q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_00.label: The 'Medical Advice' Boundary Line
q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_00.description: Families constantly ask if a symptom is normal, and you have to walk the tightrope of being helpful without practicing medicine without a license.

q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_01.label: Decoding Family Guilt Signals
q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_01.description: You can hear it in their voice when they call; sometimes they're worried about mom, but often they're calling to assuage their own guilt about not being there.

q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_02.label: Care Plan Approval Volatility
q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_02.description: Families changing minds or delaying sign-offs on care plans, forcing you to scramble schedules or risk compliance while waiting for a response.

q_02__exp_L2_01_01.label: Caregiver Reporting
q_02__exp_L2_01_01.description: Ensuring accurate information flow from the staff actually in the home.

q_02__exp_leaf_01_01_00.label: The End-of-Shift Note Rush
q_02__exp_leaf_01_01_00.description: Caregivers are clocking out and just want to go home, so they tick boxes quickly and miss the subtle change in the client's mood or appetite that matters.

q_02__exp_L1_02.label: Operational Workflow & Staffing
q_02__exp_L1_02.description: The logistical friction of maintaining continuity when staff or schedules fail.

q_02__exp_L2_02_00.label: Staffing Emergencies
q_02__exp_L2_02_00.description: Managing the immediate fallout of unexpected caregiver absences.

q_02__exp_leaf_02_00_00.label: Caregiver Call-Out Scramble
q_02__exp_leaf_02_00_00.description: A caregiver calls in sick last minute, forcing you to reassign visits and notify families instantly while the day is already starting.

q_02__exp_L1_03.label: Operational Tooling & Gaps
q_02__exp_L1_03.description: The current makeshift solutions used to bridge system failures and the ideal tools needed for efficiency.

q_02__exp_L2_03_00.label: Current Workarounds
q_02__exp_L2_03_00.description: The unofficial methods used to keep operations running when software fails.

q_02__exp_leaf_03_00_00.label: Shadow IT Communication
q_02__exp_leaf_03_00_00.description: We end up using personal text chains because the official app is too clunky to alert a caregiver about a schedule change in real-time.

q_02__exp_L2_03_01.label: Desired Efficiency
q_02__exp_L2_03_01.description: Specific technological or resource improvements that would reduce administrative drag.

q_02__exp_leaf_03_01_00.label: Integrated Real-Time Dashboard
q_02__exp_leaf_03_01_00.description: We need a single screen that shows the care plan, family contact log, and medication list together, with real-time alerts for approvals and schedule changes, without adding extra steps.

q_02__shared_root.label: Understanding the daily workflows, operational bottlenecks, and core professional challenges of elderly care workers in home visit and community center settings.
q_02__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Understanding the daily workflows, operational bottlenecks, and core professional challenges of elderly care workers in home visit and community center settings.

q_02__res_L1_00.label: Clinical Task Execution & Safety
q_02__res_L1_00.description: Investigating the specific friction points in delivering direct care tasks like medication management and physical safety monitoring.

q_02__res_L2_00_00.label: Medication Adherence Verification
q_02__res_L2_00_00.description: Understanding how workers confirm clients are taking medications correctly without constant direct observation.

q_02__res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Remote Verification Methods
q_02__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: Explore current methods used to verify intake when the worker is not physically present and the trust levels associated with them.

q_02__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Error Prevention in Complex Regimens
q_02__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Identify how workers prevent dosage errors when clients manage multiple medications with conflicting schedules.

q_02__res_L2_00_01.label: Physical Safety Monitoring
q_02__res_L2_00_01.description: Examining how immediate physical risks are assessed and mitigated during home visits or center activities.

q_02__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Real-Time Risk Detection Tools
q_02__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Determine what tools or signals workers rely on to detect fall risks or environmental hazards before an incident occurs.

q_02__res_L1_01.label: Stakeholder Communication Dynamics
q_02__res_L1_01.description: Analyzing the quality and channel appropriateness of communication regarding client health status among families and care teams.

q_02__res_L2_01_00.label: Sensitive Information Disclosure
q_02__res_L2_01_00.description: Understanding the challenges in communicating health declines or sensitive changes to family members.

q_02__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Conveying Decline Without Alarm
q_02__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Investigate how workers frame negative health updates to manage family anxiety while maintaining transparency.

q_02__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Channel Selection for Urgency Levels
q_02__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Explore how workers decide which communication channel (call, text, app) matches the urgency of the health update.

q_02__res_L2_01_01.label: Care Team Alignment
q_02__res_L2_01_01.description: Looking at how clinical observations are synced between different levels of care staff (e.g., nurses vs. aides).

q_02__res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Syncing Observations Between Roles
q_02__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Identify gaps where critical clinical observations made by aides are not effectively transferred to nursing staff.

q_02__res_L1_02.label: Tool Efficacy & Workaround Integration
q_02__res_L1_02.description: Evaluating the specific limitations of current digital tools and the features workers believe would genuinely restore care time.

q_02__res_L2_02_00.label: Digital Tool Friction Points
q_02__res_L2_02_00.description: Pinpointing specific interface or technical barriers that slow down care delivery rather than admin tasks.

q_02__res_leaf_02_00_00.label: Specific UI Barriers in Care Apps
q_02__res_leaf_02_00_00.description: Catalog specific interface elements in current EHR or care apps that cause confusion or delays during client interaction.

q_02__res_leaf_02_00_01.label: Offline vs. Online Data Sync Issues
q_02__res_leaf_02_00_01.description: Understand how connectivity issues in home settings impact data entry accuracy and workflow continuity.

q_02__res_L2_02_01.label: Ideal Support Features
q_02__res_L2_02_01.description: Defining the high-value automation or support features workers prioritize to reduce non-care burdens.

q_02__res_leaf_02_01_00.label: High-Value Automation Requests
q_02__res_leaf_02_01_00.description: Identify which specific administrative or monitoring tasks workers are willing to automate to reclaim face-to-face time.

pivot.coarse_scenario: While fixing those on-site mismatches is crucial, I also need to understand how you verify meds when you aren't there, because gaps between visits can be just as risky.

q_03__exp_L1_00.label: Senior Community Care Coordinator
q_03__exp_L1_00.description: The administrative and emotional reality of managing caregiver workflows, family expectations, and operational continuity in home and community care.

q_03__exp_L2_00_00.label: Operational & Logistics Bottlenecks
q_03__exp_L2_00_00.description: The tangible logistical friction and structural barriers that slow down care delivery and coordination.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Last-Minute Staffing Gaps
q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: When a caregiver calls in sick last minute, you're scrambling to reassign visits and figure out who can cover without overextending the team.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Dynamic Route Reassignment
q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: Rerouting everyone on the fly when the schedule gets thrown off, trying to make sure no client is left hanging or confused.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_02.label: Reactive Documentation Lag
q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_02.description: The paperwork never catches up; you're always fighting to get logs in before compliance checks or chasing forms at the end of the day.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_03.label: Safety & Plan Verification
q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_03.description: Worrying about missed medication changes or coverage gaps while scrambling, knowing one mistake could flag the system or harm a client.

q_03__exp_L2_00_01.label: Cognitive & Physical Strain
q_03__exp_L2_00_01.description: The mental load and physical exhaustion required to maintain safety and morale in a high-stimulus, reactive environment.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Constant Reactive Juggling
q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: It feels like running a relay race where the baton keeps getting dropped, or trying to keep a hundred plates spinning at once.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Field-Center Coordination Fatigue
q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: The physical strain of constant back-and-forth between the office and the field, always on your feet and thinking three steps ahead.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_02.label: Family & Client Interruption Management
q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_02.description: Handling family calls at 3 PM asking for things outside the plan without derailing the rest of the day's coordination.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_03.label: Caregiver Capacity Monitoring
q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_03.description: Keeping an eye on how the caregivers are holding up while managing the schedule, knowing if they burn out, the system falls apart.

q_04__shared_root.label: Understanding the daily workflows, operational bottlenecks, and core professional challenges of elderly care workers in home visit and community center settings.
q_04__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Understanding the daily workflows, operational bottlenecks, and core professional challenges of elderly care workers in home visit and community center settings.

q_04__exp_L1_00.label: Senior Community Care Coordinator
q_04__exp_L1_00.description: Manages complex caregiver workflows and daily operations, possessing deep knowledge of administrative burdens, emotional labor, and logistical challenges.

q_04__exp_L2_00_00.label: The "Billable Hour" Myth
q_04__exp_L2_00_00.description: Logistical realities of travel, access, and physical flow that systems assume are negligible but consume significant unpaid time.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: The "Last 100 Feet" Delay
q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: You plan for a 1-hour visit, but spend 15 minutes just trying to park, find the building buzzer, and wait for the elevator in a senior high-rise.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: The "Key Hunt" Variance
q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: The lockbox code changed yesterday, or the family moved the spare key, so you're standing outside guessing codes while the clock runs on your paid visit time.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_02.label: The "Bathroom Stop" Buffer
q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_02.description: You arrive ready to work, but the client immediately needs the toilet, and you can't leave them alone, so your 30-minute task window shrinks to 15.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_03.label: Context Switching Burnout
q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_03.description: Going from a dementia client who screamed at you to a physical therapy client who needs focus requires a mental reset that no software schedules in.

q_04__exp_L2_00_01.label: The Invisible Emotional Infrastructure
q_04__exp_L2_00_01.description: Relational work and emotional regulation required to maintain care quality that is never captured in task lists or forms.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: The "Car Cry" Decompression
q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: You sit in your car for 5 minutes after a hard visit just to cry or breathe so you don't walk into the next home carrying that heaviness.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Absorbing Family Projection
q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: Families often dump their guilt or frustration on you because you're the only one there, and you have to absorb it without reacting to keep the peace.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_02.label: The "Phone Put-Down" Signal
q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_02.description: If you keep typing on your tablet while they talk, they shut down; you have to instinctively know when to stop documenting and just look them in the eye.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_03.label: Diagnostic Intuition & Unspoken Cues
q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_03.description: You have to detect pain or non-adherence causes (like loneliness or anger) that clients hide and forms don't capture; it's about reading the room, not just the chart.

q_04__exp_L2_00_02.label: The System-Reality Disconnect
q_04__exp_L2_00_02.description: How digital tools and design assumptions fail to match the physical, connectivity, and information constraints of home care.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_02_00.label: The "Tablet Wall" Effect
q_04__exp_leaf_00_02_00.description: The software forces you to face a screen to check boxes, physically turning your back on the client during moments that need connection.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_02_01.label: Offline Mode Panic
q_04__exp_leaf_00_02_01.description: You're in a basement apartment with no signal, the app won't sync, and you're terrified you'll lose the visit proof or medication log.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_02_02.label: Fragmented Information Stitching
q_04__exp_leaf_00_02_02.description: Designers assume smooth data flow, but we're constantly stitching together info from voice memos, scribbled notes, and verbal reports because the system doesn't hold the messy reality.

q_04__exp_L2_00_03.label: Operational Volatility & Continuity
q_04__exp_L2_00_03.description: The logistical friction of maintaining continuity when staff, schedules, or resources fail in real-time.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_03_00.label: Real-Time Schedule Juggling
q_04__exp_leaf_00_03_00.description: When a caregiver calls in sick, you don't just reschedule; you have to figure out availability and overload instantly to keep everything from falling apart.

q_04__shared_root.label: Understanding the daily workflows, operational bottlenecks, and core professional challenges of elderly care workers in home visit and community center settings.
q_04__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Understanding the daily workflows, operational bottlenecks, and core professional challenges of elderly care workers in home visit and community center settings.

q_04__res_L1_00.label: The Invisible Labor & Cognitive Load Gap
q_04__res_L1_00.description: Exploring the mental and emotional work that exists outside of documented tasks, which designers often fail to account for in workflow models.

q_04__res_L2_00_00.label: Unrecorded Emotional & Mental Work
q_04__res_L2_00_00.description: Investigating the hidden emotional regulation and memory work required to deliver care effectively.

q_04__res_leaf_00_00_00.label: The 'Pre-Task' Emotional Buffer
q_04__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: Designers assume tasks are transactional; experts need to investigate the time spent de-escalating client anxiety before physical care can even begin.

q_04__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Implicit Preference Tracking
q_04__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Uncover the cognitive burden of remembering unrecorded client preferences that determine care success but are never entered into the system.

q_04__res_L2_00_01.label: Workflow Interruption Patterns
q_04__res_L2_00_01.description: Understanding how unpredictable human needs invalidate linear software workflows designed for efficiency.

q_04__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Non-Linear Reality vs. Linear Logs
q_04__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Identify where rigid digital workflows break because designers overlook the necessity of pausing care to address unpredictable human moments.

q_04__res_L1_01.label: The Physical-Digital Divide in Care Environments
q_04__res_L1_01.description: Examining the mismatch between idealized technology use cases and the messy physical reality of home and community care settings.

q_04__res_L2_01_00.label: Physical Constraints on Interaction
q_04__res_L2_01_00.description: Identifying environmental and bodily factors that designers assume are ideal but render standard devices unusable.

q_04__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: The 'Dirty Hands' Problem
q_04__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Investigate how hygiene protocols and messy home environments (poor lighting, gloves) make touchscreens impractical during critical care moments.

q_04__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Attention Splitting During Care
q_04__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Explore the safety risks designers overlook when requiring workers to switch attention between physical care tasks and digital documentation mid-task.

q_04__res_L2_01_01.label: Data Fidelity vs. Lived Reality
q_04__res_L2_01_01.description: Understanding where official digital records lag behind or contradict the sensory reality experts observe upon entry.

q_04__res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Sensory Data vs. Entered Data
q_04__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Identify critical health indicators experts detect via smell, sound, or sight that have no input field in current monitoring systems.

q_04__res_L1_02.label: Ethical Rigidity vs. Adaptive Care
q_04__res_L1_02.description: Analyzing how system constraints force workers into ethical dilemmas that designers did not anticipate.

q_04__res_L2_02_00.label: Protocol vs. Compassion Conflicts
q_04__res_L2_02_00.description: Investigating where rigid system requirements force workers to choose between compliance and client comfort.

q_04__res_leaf_02_00_00.label: Compliance-Comfort Trade-offs
q_04__res_leaf_02_00_00.description: Reveal how mandatory logging requirements can undermine client dignity, forcing workers to hide care adaptations from the system.

q_04__res_leaf_02_00_01.label: System Grace Periods
q_04__res_leaf_02_00_01.description: Determine the overlooked need for flexibility in deadlines and alerts that accounts for human variability and emergency care situations.
